participants.name: 한양대학교에서
participants.professional_persona: 한양대학교에서 기계공학 박사학위를 받고, 서울 소재 BK21 교육연구팀에서 연구교수로 재직 중입니다. 로봇 매니퓰레이터의 강인 제어 알고리즘 개발에 주력하고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 임베디드 시스템 프로그래밍과 ROS 기반 소프트웨어 아키텍처 설계에 전문성을 가지며, 다수의 로봇 경진대회 수상 경력이 있습니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: BK21 지원 기간 동안 원천 기술 특허를 출원하고 스타트업을 창업하여, 국내 제조 현장에 적용 가능한 협동 로봇을 상용화하는 것이 목표입니다.

participants.name: 제주 (s74e5g)
participants.professional_persona: 제주 지역 대학에서 관광학 교육연구단의 연구교수로 일하고 있습니다. 지역 특화 산업과 연계한 교육과정을 개발하고, 연구단의 온라인 인권교육 콘텐츠를 강화하는 데 기여하고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 관광경영 및 서비스 마케팅 연구, 교육 프로그램 개발, 연구단의 성과 확산을 위한 온·오프라인 포럼 운영 경험이 있습니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 교육연구단의 특성화 모델을 만들어 타 연구단의 모범 사례가 되고, 연구단의 중간 평가에서 좋은 점수를 받아 지원금을 안정적으로 확보하고 싶습니다.

participants.name: 경북대학교 (ne8kui)
participants.professional_persona: 경북대학교 전자공학 석사 과정에 재학 중이며, 반도체 설계 연구실에서 BK21 프로그램의 혜택을 받고 있습니다. 매월 70만 원의 장학금 덕분에 연구에만 몰두할 수 있었습니다. 군 경력이 있어 복학 후 적응이 어려웠지만, 연구실 동료들의 도움으로 잘 따라가고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: VLSI 설계와 FPGA 기반 시스템 개발에 능숙하며, Verilog를 이용한 디지털 회로 설계 경험이 풍부합니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 삼성전자나 SK하이닉스의 반도체 연구소에서 차세대 메모리 회로를 설계하는 엔지니어가 꿈입니다.

participants.name: 인문사회계열 (73x7mc)
participants.entity_name: Education Research Group Participants and Graduate Innovation Practitioners | KG-Gen-73x7mck8
participants.representative_type: Education Research Group Participants and Graduate Innovation Practitioners | KG-Gen-73x7mck8
participants.professional_persona: 인문사회계열 박사 과정생으로, 문화연구와 젠더 정책을 전공하고 있습니다. BK21 혁신인재양성사업에 참여하며, 매달 장학금을 받아 안정적으로 연구에 집중하고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 질적 연구 방법론에 능숙하며, 인터뷰와 텍스트 분석을 통해 사회문화 현상을 분석하는 데 강점이 있습니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 졸업 후 대학에 자리 잡아 젠더 및 문화 정책 연구를 지속하고, 후속 세대 연구자를 양성하는 교수가 되고 싶습니다.

participants.name: 충남대학교에서
participants.professional_persona: 충남대학교에서 약학 박사학위를 취득하고, 대전 지역 BK21 교육연구팀에서 박사후연구원으로 재직 중입니다. 단백질 분해 표적화 키메라(PROTAC)를 이용한 항암 신약 후보 물질 개발을 연구하고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 약물 설계 소프트웨어 활용과 세포 기반 약물 효능 평가에 능숙하며, 동물 실험 모델 확립에도 경험이 있습니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 국내 제약사 연구소로 진출하여 실제 신약 개발 파이프라인에 참여하고, 난치암 치료제 개발에 기여하는 것을 희망합니다.

initial_posts.narrative: As a research professor in a tourism education research group at a Jeju university, I see the BK21 program's total funding for 2022 as stated in the policy context: approximately 408,080 million KRW for education research group support, plus 52,896 million KRW for graduate innovation support, totaling about 460,976 million KRW for Hanyang University and other institutions. However, the policy context specifies that the total support budget for 577 education research groups in 2022 is 408,080 million KRW, including carryover. For Hanyang University specifically, I estimate the allocated funding based on its share of groups. Hanyang has a strong presence in science and engineering, likely hosting around 20-30 groups out of 577. Given the total budget of 408,080 million KRW and the fact that Hanyang is a top university, I estimate its total funding allocated at about 408,080 million KRW (the national total for education research groups) because the policy context does not break it down per university, and I assume Hanyang's allocation is a significant portion. For graduate scholarship slots, the policy states about 12,600 graduate students per year in future talent training and 6,400 in innovation talent training, totaling 19,000 nationally. Hanyang's share would be proportional to its number of groups. I estimate 19,000 slots nationally, with Hanyang hosting about 10% of groups, so roughly 1,900 slots for Hanyang, but the prediction field asks for the total slots funded, not per university. Given the ambiguity, I use the national total of 19,000 as the number of monthly stipend slots funded. For postdoc positions, the policy mentions early-career researchers receiving a minimum salary of 3 million KRW per month. With a total budget of 408,080 million KRW and assuming 10% goes to postdoc salaries (about 40,808 million KRW), at 36 million KRW per year per postdoc, that yields about 1,134 postdocs nationally. However, considering the policy context states 577 groups and each group may have 2-3 postdocs, I estimate 3,000 postdoc positions nationally. I base this on my experience in a tourism group where we have 2 postdocs, and larger groups in science may have more. Thus, I estimate 3,000 postdoc positions funded nationally.
evidence: The policy context states the total annual budget for education research group support is approximately 408,080 million KRW in 2022, which I use as the total funding allocated for Hanyang's BK21 program since no per-university breakdown is given.

The policy context mentions about 12,600 graduate students per year in future talent training and 6,400 in innovation talent training, totaling 19,000 monthly stipend slots nationally, which I adopt as the graduate_scholarship_slots value.

Based on the minimum postdoc salary of 3 million KRW per month and typical group sizes, I estimate 3,000 postdoc positions nationally, consistent with 577 groups averaging 5 postdocs each, though my tourism group has fewer.

initial_posts.narrative: As a humanities and social sciences doctoral student at Hanyang University, I estimate that the BK21 program will support 19,000 graduate students and 3,000 postdocs in 2022, based on the policy's stated slots and typical group sizes. For publications, I reason that each graduate student and postdoc produces about 1-2 publications per year on average, given the emphasis on research output in the program. With 22,000 total supported researchers (19,000 + 3,000), this yields roughly 38,000 publications. International conference presentations are less common in my field, but the program encourages internationalization; I estimate about 20% of publications are presented at international conferences, leading to 7,600 presentations. These estimates reflect my experience in a cultural studies group where we have 2 postdocs and each student produces about 1-2 papers annually, with some presenting at international conferences. The prior phases confirm the funding and slot numbers, so I use those as anchors.

initial_posts.narrative: As an early-career researcher at Chungnam National University in a PROTAC group, I see the outcomes phase building on the prior phases. The inputs established 19,000 graduate slots and 3,000 postdoc positions, with total funding of 460,976 million KRW. Activities ensured funds were disbursed (408,080 million KRW), evaluation panels convened (3), consulting sessions held (280), and compliance checks executed (205). Outputs included 33,000 publications and 6,600 international conference presentations. For outcomes, I estimate the international research collaboration rate of BK21 participants in 2022 at 6,600, based on the number of international conference presentations from the outputs phase, which reflects active collaboration. The graduate employment rate of BK21 participants in 2022 I estimate at 15,000, assuming about 79% of the 19,000 graduate students find employment within one year, consistent with the policy's target of 86.7% adjusted for current trends. The employment rate is 78.9%, influenced by network effects from consulting and evaluation feedback loops that improve training quality. The international collaboration rate is 30%, driven by program incentives like international conference support and collaboration requirements. The full-time faculty lecture ratio is 85%, reflecting increased reliance on full-time faculty due to program design elements that prioritize research mentoring and educational quality. Evaluation and consulting feedback loops contribute by identifying weaknesses and promoting best practices, enhancing these outcomes.

The full-time faculty lecture ratio of 85% reflects the program's emphasis on full-time faculty for research mentoring, as seen in my university where most graduate courses are taught by full-time professors, improving educational quality.

initial_posts.narrative: As an early-career researcher at Chungnam National University in a PROTAC group, I see the impact phase as the long-term result of sustained investment in graduate talent development. Over a decade, the BK21 program's consistent funding of 19,000 graduate slots and 3,000 postdoc positions per year, combined with evaluation feedback loops and consulting, builds a critical mass of highly trained researchers. These alumni, now in academia and industry, drive national innovation capacity. The dissemination of outstanding group achievements through the BK21 webzine and forums spreads best practices in research integrity, collaboration, and interdisciplinarity across institutions. Based on my experience, South Korea's rank in global S&T talent competitiveness has improved from around 10th to 8th, reflecting the program's cumulative effect. For high-impact publications, I estimate 12.5 per 1000 researchers, derived from the outputs phase (33,000 publications from 22,000 researchers) but focusing on top 1% cited works. In my field, about 1-2% of papers become highly cited, but with BK21's emphasis on quality and collaboration, I estimate 1.25% of publications are top-cited, yielding 412.5 top-cited papers from 33,000 publications. Per 1000 researchers (22,000 total), that's 18.75, but I adjust downward to 12.5 because not all 22,000 researchers are equally productive in high-impact work—many master's students and early-stage researchers contribute less. The systemic changes in research culture, such as mandatory online education on human rights and ethics, and the push for international collaboration (30% collaboration rate), further enhance the quality of research outputs over time.
evidence: The BK21 program's sustained investment in 19,000 graduate slots and 3,000 postdoc positions per year over a decade builds a large pool of highly trained researchers who contribute to national innovation capacity, improving South Korea's global S&T talent competitiveness rank from around 10th to 8th.

From the outputs phase, 33,000 publications from 22,000 researchers yield an average of 1.5 publications per researcher, but only about 1.25% of these are top 1% cited, giving 412.5 high-impact publications, which translates to 18.75 per 1000 researchers, but I adjust to 12.5 considering lower productivity in early-career and humanities researchers.

The dissemination of outstanding group achievements through the BK21 webzine and forums spreads best practices in research integrity, collaboration, and interdisciplinarity, leading to systemic improvements in research culture across institutions over time.

My own experience in a PROTAC group shows that international collaboration (30% rate) and evaluation feedback loops enhance research quality, but the impact on high-impact publications is gradual, as many researchers are still in early career stages and not yet producing top-cited work.
